messages.content: suppose L(\theta) = -\mathbb{E}_{a \sim \pi_\theta}[R(a) \cdot \log \pi_\theta(a)], what’s the equation of the \nabla_\theta L?

Let \(\pi_\theta(a)\) be a differentiable probability mass/density and \(R(a)\) not depend on \(\theta\). Write the expectation explicitly:

\[
L(\theta)=-\mathbb{E}_{a\sim \pi_\theta}\big[R(a)\log \pi_\theta(a)\big]
= -\int \pi_\theta(a)\, R(a)\, \log \pi_\theta(a)\, da.
\]

Differentiate under the integral:

\[
\nabla_\theta L(\theta)
= -\int \nabla_\theta\!\Big(\pi_\theta(a)\, R(a)\, \log \pi_\theta(a)\Big)\, da.
\]

Using \(\nabla_\theta(\pi \log \pi)= (\nabla_\theta \pi)(\log \pi + 1)\),

\[
\nabla_\theta L(\theta)
= -\int R(a)\, (\nabla_\theta \pi_\theta(a))\,(\log \pi_\theta(a)+1)\, da.
\]

Use the score-function identity \(\nabla_\theta \pi_\theta(a)=\pi_\theta(a)\nabla_\theta \log \pi_\theta(a)\):

\[
\boxed{
\nabla_\theta L(\theta)
= -\mathbb{E}_{a\sim \pi_\theta}\Big[ R(a)\,(\log \pi_\theta(a)+1)\,\nabla_\theta \log \pi_\theta(a)\Big].
}
\]

Equivalently,

\[
\nabla_\theta L(\theta)
= -\mathbb{E}_{a\sim \pi_\theta}\big[ R(a)\log \pi_\theta(a)\,\nabla_\theta \log \pi_\theta(a)\big]
\;-\;
\mathbb{E}_{a\sim \pi_\theta}\big[ R(a)\,\nabla_\theta \log \pi_\theta(a)\big].
\]
